title: Create .memory/ directory in setupJobWorkspace()
spec:
## Task

Add `.memory/` directory scaffolding to `setupJobWorkspace()` in `packages/local-agent/src/workspace.ts`.

### What to build

In `setupJobWorkspace()` (around line 422), after creating the workspace directory and before writing other files:

1. Create `.memory/` at the workspace root (i.e. `join(config.workspaceDir, '.memory')`). Use `mkdirSync` with `{ recursive: true }` — this is safe to call even if the directory already exists.
2. Seed an empty `MEMORY.md` at `.memory/MEMORY.md` — but **only if the file does not already exist** (`existsSync` guard). The file should be empty (zero bytes).

### Key constraint
Existing memory files must NEVER be overwritten. This block only runs when the file is absent. The `.memory/` directory itself is safe to `mkdirSync` unconditionally — it won't wipe existing contents.

### Files
- `packages/local-agent/src/workspace.ts` — `setupJobWorkspace()` function

### What NOT to do
- Do not touch `.claude/memory/` — that is the existing heartbeat memory system and must not be changed.
- Do not add any new exports or change the function signature.
- Do not seed any files other than `MEMORY.md`.
acceptance_tests:
Feature: .memory/ directory setup

  Scenario: Fresh workspace creation creates .memory/ and MEMORY.md
    Given setupJobWorkspace() is called for a new workspace directory
    When the function completes
    Then .memory/ directory exists at the workspace root
    And .memory/MEMORY.md exists
    And .memory/MEMORY.md is empty

  Scenario: Existing memory files are not overwritten
    Given .memory/MEMORY.md already exists with content
    When setupJobWorkspace() is called again for the same workspace
    Then .memory/MEMORY.md still contains the original content

  Scenario: Existing .claude/memory/ is unaffected
    Given a workspace with an existing .claude/memory/ directory
    When setupJobWorkspace() is called
    Then .claude/memory/ contents are unchanged

title: Add memory instructions to boot prompt and persistent agent CLAUDE.md
spec:
## Task

Update the boot prompt and CLAUDE.md content for persistent agents to instruct the agent about the `.memory/` system.

### Changes required

#### 1. DEFAULT_BOOT_PROMPT (executor.ts, around line 331)

Update the constant to prepend a `.memory/MEMORY.md` read instruction:

```
If .memory/MEMORY.md exists, read it and load relevant memories before doing anything else. 
Read your state files. If .reports/{role}-report.md exists, review it for continuity. Check for pending work via your MCP tools. Orient yourself and begin.
```

#### 2. CLAUDE.md memory instructions section (workspace.ts)

Add a new section to the CLAUDE.md generated for persistent agents that explains the `.memory/` system. The section should be appended to `claudeMdContent` for persistent roles (i.e. when `config.heartbeatMd !== undefined`), or injected via a helper in `setupJobWorkspace()`.

The section content should explain:
- Memory lives at `.memory/` in the workspace root
- `MEMORY.md` is the index — one line per entry, max 150 chars, max 200 lines
- Each memory is its own file with frontmatter: `name`, `description`, `type`, plus body content
- Four types: `user`, `feedback`, `project`, `reference`
- Write memory inline during conversation when something notable comes up (decisions, corrections, preferences, context)
- Check for existing memory on the topic before creating a new file — update over duplicate
- No memory for routine ops (standups, pipeline queries, status checks)
- On idle sync prompt: review memories, remove stale entries, merge near-duplicates

### Files
- `packages/local-agent/src/executor.ts` — `DEFAULT_BOOT_PROMPT` constant
- `packages/local-agent/src/workspace.ts` — `setupJobWorkspace()` or a new helper

### What NOT to do
- Do not remove or break the existing Memory Maintenance section (`MEMORY_MAINTENANCE_SECTION`) — this is for the `.claude/memory/` heartbeat files and must remain.
- Do not change the function signatures.
- The new memory section should only be added for persistent agents (gated on `heartbeatMd !== undefined`).
acceptance_tests:
Feature: Memory instructions in boot prompt and CLAUDE.md

  Scenario: Boot prompt instructs agent to read MEMORY.md
    Given DEFAULT_BOOT_PROMPT is evaluated
    Then the prompt contains an instruction to read .memory/MEMORY.md
    And the original state-file and MCP check instructions are still present

  Scenario: Persistent agent CLAUDE.md includes memory system instructions
    Given setupJobWorkspace() is called with heartbeatMd defined
    When the CLAUDE.md is written
    Then it contains a section describing the .memory/ system
    And it includes instructions for the four memory types
    And it includes the write-inline and update-over-duplicate rules

  Scenario: Ephemeral job CLAUDE.md is unchanged
    Given setupJobWorkspace() is called with heartbeatMd undefined
    When the CLAUDE.md is written
    Then it does NOT contain the .memory/ memory system section

title: Idle-triggered memory sync nudge in persistent agent heartbeat
acceptance_tests:
Feature: Idle-triggered memory sync nudge

  Scenario: Nudge fires after 5 minutes of inactivity
    Given a persistent agent has been idle for 5+ minutes (pane hash unchanged)
    And lastMemorySyncAt is null
    When the heartbeat fires
    Then tmux send-keys injects the memory sync prompt
    And lastMemorySyncAt is set to the current timestamp

  Scenario: Nudge does not fire repeatedly during extended idle
    Given a persistent agent has been idle for 10+ minutes
    And lastMemorySyncAt was set 5 minutes ago
    When the heartbeat fires
    Then no additional send-keys injection occurs

  Scenario: Nudge resets when agent becomes active
    Given a persistent agent received a nudge (lastMemorySyncAt is set)
    When the pane hash changes (agent is actively responding)
    Then lastMemorySyncAt is reset to null
    And wasActiveAfterSync is set to true

  Scenario: Nudge does not fire while reset is in progress
    Given resetInProgress is true
    And the agent has been idle for 5+ minutes
    When the heartbeat fires
    Then no send-keys injection occurs

  Scenario: Nudge does not fire before 5 minutes idle
    Given a persistent agent has been idle for 4 minutes
    When the heartbeat fires
    Then no send-keys injection occurs
